title: When the Sensor Starts Thinking: SnortML, Agentic AI, and the Evolving Architecture of Intrusion Detection

title: Terraform adds cost visibility, project-level notifications, and more
summary: In the past few months, the HashiCorp Terraform engineering team has continuously improved features to help organizations eliminate infrastructure blind spots and strengthen governance and security across the entire infrastructure lifecycle. sections.overview:
In the past few months, the HashiCorp Terraform engineering team has continuously improved features to help organizations eliminate infrastructure blind spots and strengthen governance and security across the entire infrastructure lifecycle. The latest HCP Terraform and Terraform Enterprise improvements include:

 ·       Billable resource analytics (GA)

 ·       Project-level remote state sharing (GA)

 ·       Module testing for dynamic credentials (GA)

 ·       Project-level notification (GA)

 ·       Registry tagging (Beta)

 Billable resource analytics Feature gap: Organizations using resources under management  (RUM)-based billing face a significant visibility challenge when trying to understand where infrastructure costs are coming from. Until now, HCP Terraform customers could only view their total billable managed resources at the organization level.
sections.technical_breakdown:
Project-level notification Feature gap: Until now, platform teams managing large-scale infrastructure on HCP Terraform faced a significant operational hurdle when configuring alerts and observability.

This feature allows you to use projects as a centralized control plane to define your observability standards, ensuring that users have an effective, automated way to monitor their infrastructure at scale.

They were limited to two primary options: sharing state with every workspace in the entire organization, which increased the security risk, or manually managing a list of workspaces, which was slow and error-prone.

They were required to configure notification settings, such as Slack webhooks, PagerDuty triggers, or email alerts manually on a workspace-by-workspace basis.

This gap often led to "silent failures," situations where critical errors in newly provisioned environments went completely unnoticed by operations teams because the workspace creator forgot to configure the necessary alerts.
sections.impact:
This gap forced many customers into an unsustainable "multi-organization" model — creating numerous separate organizations just to maintain security boundaries — which is difficult to manage and impacts system performance. Until now, HCP Terraform customers could only view their total billable managed resources at the organization level. What's new: We are introducing the general availability of billable resource analytics for HCP Terraform. This new capability transforms how organizations manage infrastructure costs by providing users with comprehensive visibility into resource consumption across their entire organization.

Classified impact band: high. Affected layer signal: cloud — aws.
sections.resolution: Resolution detail may be partial in the RSS excerpt. Refer to the original write-up for timelines, mitigations, and verification steps.
sections.lessons_learned:
In the past few months, the HashiCorp Terraform engineering team has continuously improved features to help organizations eliminate infrastructure blind spots and strengthen governance and security across the entire infrastructure lifecycle.

The latest HCP Terraform and Terraform Enterprise improvements include:

 ·       Billable resource analytics (GA)

 ·       Project-level remote state sharing (GA)

 ·       Module testing for dynamic credentials (GA)

 ·       Project-level notification (GA)

 ·       Registry tagging (Beta)

 Billable resource analytics Feature gap: Organizations using resources under management  (RUM)-based billing face a significant visibility challenge when trying to understand where infrastructure costs are coming from.

This consolidation leads to better performance, easier reporting, and a more cohesive management experience for administrators.
sections.root_cause_highlight: Likely root-cause theme: control plane issue. This feature allows you to use projects as a true isolation boundary so that users can have an effective way to control their state sharing, increasing security and developer velocity. The tfe_outputs data source is more secure because it does not require full access to workspace state to fetch outputs. This gap often led to "silent failures," situations where critical errors in newly provisioned environments went completely unnoticed by operations teams because the workspace creator forgot to configure the necessary alerts.

sections.trends:
Large-scale outages remain the forcing function for resilient architecture, communication, and automation investments.

Observability shifts remain tightly coupled with incident response quality and capacity planning accuracy.

Demand spikes and capacity cliffs continue to drive autoscaling, queueing, and graceful degradation designs.

title: Tekton joins the CNCF as an incubating project
summary: Tekton has joined the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) as an incubating project. For years, practitioners have used Tekton as the foundation for Kubernetes-native CI/CD. This transition from the Continuous Delivery Foundation (CDF) to the CNCF aligns the project even more closely with the broader Kubernetes ecosystem and the community it serves.
timeline_entries: Enterprise readiness: We help ensure Tekton meets the security and scalability needs of the world's largest organizations.
Increased security: The project will use CNCF security audits and best practices to further harden the software supply chain.

sections.overview: Tekton has joined the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) as an incubating project. For years, practitioners have used Tekton as the foundation for Kubernetes-native CI/CD. This transition from the Continuous Delivery Foundation (CDF) to the CNCF aligns the project even more closely with the broader Kubernetes ecosystem and the community it serves.
sections.technical_breakdown:
For years, practitioners have used Tekton as the foundation for Kubernetes-native CI/CD.

This transition from the Continuous Delivery Foundation (CDF) to the CNCF aligns the project even more closely with the broader Kubernetes ecosystem and the community it serves.

Tekton was created to meet the need for a CI/CD framework that doesn't just run on Kubernetes—it is built for Kubernetes.

Joining the foundation that hosts Kubernetes, Argo CD, and Helm integrates the project more closely with standardized delivery.

User experience: We developed the Tekton Dashboard and CLI (tkn) to make Tekton more accessible to developers.
sections.impact:
User experience: We developed the Tekton Dashboard and CLI (tkn) to make Tekton more accessible to developers. Impact on the ecosystem Joining the CNCF benefits the entire open source community.

Classified impact band: medium. Affected layer signal: platform — kubernetes.
sections.resolution: Resolution detail may be partial in the RSS excerpt. Refer to the original write-up for timelines, mitigations, and verification steps.
sections.lessons_learned:
This support aligns with our strategy to use open standards to prevent vendor lock-in in the CI/CD space.

We will work with the community to keep Tekton a primary choice for cloud-native delivery.
sections.incident_timeline:
Enterprise readiness: We help ensure Tekton meets the security and scalability needs of the world's largest organizations.
Increased security: The project will use CNCF security audits and best practices to further harden the software supply chain.
sections.trends:
Large-scale outages remain the forcing function for resilient architecture, communication, and automation investments.

Observability shifts remain tightly coupled with incident response quality and capacity planning accuracy.

email_summary: Tekton has joined the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) as an incubating project. For years, practitioners have used Tekton as the foundation for Kubernetes-native CI/CD.
